title: Segfault on a failed image pull
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**
CI found a segfault during a (presumed) network flake
 
**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Run the CI

2. Have the universe choose to fail at a perfect moment?


**Describe the results you received:**
+0227s] === RUN   TestImage_NewFromLocal
[+0228s] Trying to pull docker.io/library/busybox:latest...

**Describe the results you expected:**
probably just the image pull fails gracefully

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
latest master
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
remote: false
fedora 30
root
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
this should be the link to the CI run: https://cirrus-ci.com/task/5183568630775808

comments.body: This issue had no activity for 30 days. In the absence of activity or the "do-not-close" label, the issue will be automatically closed within 7 days.
Since we have not seen this again, I am going to close, @haircommander reopen if this is something you feel we should pursue.
